With fresh corn, cherry tomatoes, and basil, this pasta is a celebration of all things summer, but it's the corn 'pesto' — a sauce made with sautéed corn, garlic, Parmesan, and olive oil — that really makes it special. {"title": "Greek Split Pea Spread", "summary": "Originally, this Greek spread called fava (which is similar to hummus) was made out of fava beans—hence its name. With time, the beans were replaced with dried yellow split peas grown in Santorini, considered the best of their kind because of the island’s volcanic soil. The peas are rich in plant-based protein, nutritious fiber, vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. If you buy the dried peas in a sealed package, choose one with an expiration date as far out as possible, and if you buy the peas in bulk, be sure there is high turnover at the store. The cooking time for dried peas that aren’t fresh is much longer, and they have an off-putting aftertaste.", "recipe_type": "Lunch", "dietary_tags": ["vegan", "vegetarian"], "cuisine_tags": ["Greek", "Mediterranean"], "cook_time_text": "90 mins", "ingredients_text": "dried yellow split peas, water, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, pepper", "instructions_text": "1. Blend the peas with olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and pepper until smooth. 5. Adjust seasoning to taste. 6. Serve chilled or at room temperature. 7. Store in an airtight container in the fridge. 8. Enjoy as a spread or dip."}

{"title": "Greek Eggplant Spread", "summary": "The Greeks consider this dish a salad, but its consistency is much more akin to a spread. The roasted eggplant is then chopped by hand (never in a food processor!) to retain a chunky texture, ensuring that every bite has a slightly different mouthfeel than the one before.", "recipe_type": "Lunch", "dietary_tags": ["gluten-free", "vegan"], "cuisine_tags": ["Greek", "Mediterranean"], "cook_time_text": "50 mins", "ingredients_text": "eggplant, olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, salt, pepper", "instructions_text": "1. Adjust seasoning to taste. 6. Serve as a spread with bread or crackers. 7. Store leftovers in the refrigerator. 8. Enjoy with fresh vegetables."}

{"title": "Butter Bean, Sungold, And Smashed Cucumber Salad", "summary": "When sungold tomato season rolls around, I find myself tossing them in just about any dish I can. They truly are the jewels of summer! Marinating the sungolds and smashed cucumbers slightly ahead of time gives them a chance to release a bit of their juices, making a lovely, flavorful base for the salad. I find the easiest way to smash the cucumbers is by placing my knife flat on top of the cucumber and smashing down with my palm, as you would crush a garlic clove. I call for celery vinegar here (TART Vinegar makes one) because I find it adds such a special touch to this salad. However, red wine vinegar is lovely, too, and a more readily available swap. {"title": "Fried Squash Blossom \"Mozzarella Sticks\"", "summary": "Squash blossoms are a true gift of summer. The end result is a mash-up of a classic, creamy, cheese-filled blossom and a stringy, pull-y mozzarella stick. Truly a delight! {"title": "Traditional Greek Salad", "summary": "This Greek salad is a true summer classic, loaded with the best flavors of the Mediterranean. The name horiatiki comes from the Greek word for “village,” and while similar salads have been around since the 1800s, the modern version with feta didn’t appear until the 1960s. Back then, taverna owners in Athens added a block of feta to dodge government-set salad prices, which made this dish a star. Today, it’s all about using perfectly ripe, juicy tomatoes—if you can’t find them, it’s worth waiting.
